headline: Engineer Brings AI Education to Underserved Communities
meta_description: A former NASA spacecraft engineer is working to ensure artificial intelligence education reaches those historically left behind by the tech revolution.
summary_short: After two decades building spacecraft, engineer Nia Jetter now focuses on making AI education accessible to underserved communities.
body:
Nia Jetter spent two decades as an engineer designing and building spacecraft and robots—cutting-edge work at the frontier of technology. But as artificial intelligence began reshaping society, she recognized a critical problem: the benefits and opportunities of the AI revolution were not reaching everyone equally. Those without access to quality AI education risked being left further behind.

Jetter made a significant shift in her career, leaving advanced aerospace work to focus on a more fundamental challenge: bringing AI education to people and communities that have historically been excluded from access to technical training and opportunity. Her goal is straightforward but ambitious—ensuring that the AI revolution does not deepen existing divides.

This transition reflects a growing awareness that technological progress only serves humanity fully when it is broadly accessible. By dedicating her expertise to closing gaps in AI education, Jetter is helping to build a foundation where more people can participate in and benefit from one of the defining technologies of our time.
